reasoning: The most recent known value for the Treasury General Account (WTREGEN) as of the forecast date (2026-02-01) is 923,042 million from January 28, 2026. This is a very high balance, elevated by mid-January Q4 estimated tax receipts. Historically, the TGA reliably drops in the first week of February due to start-of-month government outlays (e.g. a drop of ~40k in early Feb 2024). Furthermore, the Treasury begins issuing tax refunds in February and March, which consistently drains the cash balance. Importantly, the US Treasury's most recent Quarterly Refunding Announcement sets a target end-of-March 2026 cash balance of $850 billion, which is well below the current $923 billion level, meaning debt issuance will be managed to allow the balance to decline over the quarter. Therefore, it is highly unlikely the weekly average will increase above 923,042 by Feb 8 or Mar 3. Following instructions, the probability is set lower for the near term and relaxed closer to 0.5 for the more distant date to reflect widening horizon variance.
